description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the Docker image registry.gitlab.com/multicloud-openstack-k8s/docker-kubespray-ansible with stages up, down, pages, and deploy. Define a .deploy template that runs on the master branch, is manual, and in the up stage; its before_script applies the kubespray‑v2.6.0.patch, removes world‑write permissions, creates a 700 ~/.ssh directory, writes $SSH_PUBLIC_KEY to id_rsa.pub, starts ssh‑agent, adds $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, installs Python requirements from gitlab/requirements.txt, exports OS_PASSWORD from the environment‑specific variable and aborts if empty, then the script executes ./up.sh $CI_ENVIRONMENT_NAME. Define a .stop template that also runs only on master in the down stage; its before_script creates ~/.ssh, writes $ID_RSA_PUB to id_rsa.pub, exports OS_PASSWORD similarly and aborts if empty, and its script runs ./down.sh $CI_ENVIRONMENT_NAME. For each city (amsterdam, amsterdam2, berlin, buffalo, dubai, frankfurt2, frankfurt3, frankfurt4, gravelines, karlskrona, london, london2, milano, montreal, montreal2, oslo, paris, prague, stockholm, stockholm2, strasburg, tokyo, warsaw) create a deploy_<city> job that extends .deploy, sets environment name to the city and on_stop to the matching stop_<city> job, and a stop_<city> job that extends .stop, sets environment action to stop and name to the city. Add a pages job using the python image, only on master, that installs pyaml and jinja2‑cli, generates map/clouds.json, copies the map directory to public, renders app.gitlab-ci.yml.j2 with clouds.yaml into public/app.gitlab-ci.yml, and publishes the public/ directory as an artifact.
